frontierComparison.interpretation: The open ledger count increased by recording the bulk-classification object, but the p479-available residue set token was consumed: 1140 residue classes now have no survivors and are grouped into 30 finite obstruction-prime buckets. This is still not global convergence until the buckets are compressed, terminally bounded, or recombined with the remaining complements.

assembledPieces.claim: The p443 availability split exposed the selected q97 square-obstruction child and left the p443-unavailable complement open.
assembledPieces.boundary: It does not close the p443-unavailable complement or wider p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: The p151 route is ledgered as blocked/open, and the p479 reserve is exact-certified as a squarefree representative repair before any deeper selector descent.
assembledPieces.boundary: Representative repair is not subclass coverage.

assembledPieces.claim: The selected q97 child is partitioned by the p479 availability formula k_479(w) = (131596*w + 250) mod 229441 into 1140 available and 228301 unavailable residues.
assembledPieces.boundary: The split itself does not close the available residues, unavailable complement, q97 child, or p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: All 1140 p479-available residue classes are classified by square-obstruction children with no survivor residues, using obstruction primes up to q=173.
assembledPieces.boundary: The square-obstruction children are not individually closed; they are now grouped for bucket compression.

assembledPieces.claim: The 1140 covered residues group into 30 obstruction-prime buckets.
assembledPieces.boundary: A histogram is not yet a theorem over the bucket families.

nextFiniteToken.rankDecreaseIfCompleted: The p479-available branch is replaced by 30 theorem-facing bucket families or exact per-bucket boundaries instead of 1140 separate residue rows or a singleton q127 descent.

bucketCompressionCandidate.name: p479_available_obstruction_prime_bucket_theorem
bucketCompressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All square-obstruction children emitted by the p479-available residue bulk cover for the selected p443/q97 child.
bucketCompressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The no-survivor bulk cover transformed a residue-level finite screen into 30 repeated obstruction-prime mechanisms. Compressing by bucket is the smallest theorem-facing move that can reduce the repeated child shape without opening q127 or another singleton child.
bucketCompressionCandidate.whyNotSingleQChildDescent: A q127-only repair would handle one bucket of 97 residues while leaving 29 other buckets and the p479-unavailable complement unassembled.

bucketBoundaryPlan.ifCompressionSucceeds: Emit a theorem packet proving a shared square-obstruction family or finite boundary for each obstruction-prime bucket.
bucketBoundaryPlan.ifCompressionFails: Emit exact bucket-boundary packets listing the bucket prime, residue-class count, root-congruence shape, any nonuniform rows, and the deterministic successor denominator or blocker.
bucketBoundaryPlan.forbiddenFallback: Do not descend into q127 or any singleton q-bucket unless the compression packet proves it consumes a finite bucket-rank token.

oneNextAction.action: Compress the 30 p479-available obstruction-prime buckets into theorem-facing families, or emit exact bucket-boundary packets without q127 singleton descent.
oneNextAction.coveredFamily: The complete 30-bucket square-obstruction classification of all 1140 p479-available residue classes for the selected p443/q97 child.
oneNextAction.finiteDenominatorOrRankToken: Finite token p443_q97_p479_available_obstruction_prime_buckets: 30 buckets, 1140 total residue classes, period 479^2.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If a shared bucket theorem is unavailable, emit exact per-bucket boundary packets with bucket prime, residue-class count, nonuniform root shapes, and the deterministic successor/blocker for that bucket.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The bucket set is finite and already classified; compressing 30 buckets attacks the repeated child shape directly, while q127 descent would reopen the sibling-ladder anti-pattern.
oneNextAction.completionRule: A bucket-compression packet proves a shared theorem over the obstruction buckets, or emits exact per-bucket boundaries that consume a finite bucket-rank token.

coversPrimaryNextAction.reason: This assembly is specifically for the current bucket-compression primary action after the p479-available bulk cover.

nextTheoremMove: Compress the 30 obstruction-prime buckets into theorem-facing families, or emit exact bucket-boundary packets; do not descend into q127 or any singleton q-bucket unless a finite bucket-rank token is consumed.
proofBoundary: This convergence assembly records the post-bulk-cover frontier and selects bucket compression. It does not prove a bucket theorem, close any square-obstruction bucket, close q127 or q127-avoiding children, close the p479-unavailable complement, close the q97 child, close the p443-unavailable complement, close the wider p4217 complement, prove collision-free matching, or decide Problem 848.
